title: Fix rakkib web ctx NameError
description: The recent web runtime config change added repo_dir=ctx.obj["repo_dir"] inside the web command body, but the command function does not currently receive a Click context. Running  now crashes with NameError: ctx is not defined. Update the command signature/decorators so repo_dir is available correctly and the web command works again.
close_reason: Added Click context injection to the web command so repo_dir can be read from ctx.obj without crashing.

title: Fix NocoDB database name mismatch in Postgres init SQL
close_reason: Fixed by commit 690f899

title: Fix jellyfin.caddy.tmpl site block nesting
description: jellyfin.caddy.tmpl starts with jellyfin.DOMAIN { (a site block) but is imported inside the :80 { ... } block by Caddyfile.footer.tmpl. Nesting site blocks inside site blocks is invalid Caddyfile syntax. Must rewrite as @jellyfin host / handle block like other routes.
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix registry.yaml indentation for transfer and jellyfin
description: transfer and jellyfin entries in registry.yaml are at root level instead of under services:, causing yaml.safe_load to return a list and KeyError in _load_registry.
close_reason: Closed

title: Wave 2 — render + plumbing: render.py, secrets.py, docker.py + steps
description:
Implement render.py, secrets.py, docker.py. Port Steps 10, 30, 50, 60, 80, 90. Each step has run() and verify(); verify() returns ok or a structured failure (step, log path, state slice). Docker output redirects to ${DATA_ROOT}/logs/<step>.log — no LLM watches the pull progress, fixing the token-burn issue. Idempotent re-apply on every step.

From pyplan.md: Wave 2 — render + plumbing. Implement render.py, secrets.py, docker.py. Port Steps 10, 30, 50, 60, 80, 90.
close_reason: Wave 2 complete: render.py, secrets.py, docker.py, Steps 10/30/50/60/80/90, CLI wiring, 223 passing tests

title: Wave 1 — interview: state.py, schema.py, interview.py
description:
Implement state.py, schema.py, interview.py. Read AgentSchema YAML out of each questions/*.md, drive Phases 1–6 entirely in Python. Delete the agent-prompt path for the interview. Resume is automatic: load state, find first phase with unset required keys, start there. If confirmed: true, ask once whether to start over (overwriting .fss-state.yaml). The binary owns the interview, fixing the expensive LLM-per-turn cost of v1.

From pyplan.md: Wave 1 — interview. Implement state.py, schema.py, interview.py. Read AgentSchema YAML out of each questions/*.md, drive Phases 1–6 entirely in Python. Resume is automatic: load state, find first phase with unset required keys, start there.
close_reason: Wave 1 complete: state.py, schema.py, interview.py, normalize.py, validate.py, and 125 passing tests. CLI init wired.

title: Wave 0 — bootstrap: Python + pipx install.sh
description:
Update install.sh to ensure python3 and pipx are present, then pipx install from the cloned repo. Keep the multi-shell PATH writing (bashrc/zshrc/profile) since pipx still relies on ~/.local/bin being on PATH. This is the bootstrap wave that enables the Python binary install path.

From pyplan.md: Wave 0 — bootstrap. Update install.sh to ensure python3 and pipx are present, then pipx install from the cloned repo. Keep the multi-shell PATH writing (Issue 1 from the prior plan) since pipx still relies on ~/.local/bin being on PATH.
close_reason: Wave 0 complete: install.sh now ensures python3 and pipx, pipx installs from cloned repo, multi-shell PATH writing for bashrc/zshrc/profile. Added pyproject.toml and src/rakkib/cli.py as minimal Python package for pipx compatibility. Tested end-to-end locally.

title: Refine setup phase UI cards
description: Adjust the frontend setup route UI: remove reads/writes technical blocks from phases 1,2,4,6; fold derived field content into the backend state section for each phase; rework phase 3 to select services directly from the service catalog with subdomain tags and without the old multiselect lists; remove unnecessary technical data from phase 4.
close_reason: Updated setup phase UI to merge derived backend state into the phase summary, remove technical reads/writes blocks, make phase 3 service cards directly selectable with subdomain tags, and trim phase 4 Cloudflare internals.

title: Implement web post-phase-6 run flow
description: Phase 6 currently saves successfully but stops on placeholder confirm/run screens. Implement the missing post-phase-6 web flow end-to-end: a real confirmation screen, backend endpoint to start a background rakkib pull, status/log retrieval, and a live setup run screen that reflects progress.
close_reason: Implemented the post-phase-6 web flow with a real confirmation screen, background pull runner endpoints, persisted repo-relative state handling, and a live run/log screen.

title: Simplify auth command for Docker repair
description: The auth UX should stay simple: use plain rakkib auth for auth-related setup instead of introducing rakkib auth docker. Remove the extra subcommand flow and keep Docker group repair behind the single auth command.
close_reason: Simplified Docker repair to use plain rakkib auth and removed the rakkib auth docker subcommand path.

title: Add one-command Docker access repair
description: Non-root users can recover Docker access only by copying several manual commands. Add a simple Rakkib command and improve pull-time repair so regular users can prepare Docker group access with guided sudo validation.
close_reason: Added one-command Docker access repair via rakkib auth docker and made pull attempt interactive sudo repair for non-root users.

title: Fix non-root Docker access handling
description: Rakkib pull currently stops with Docker installed but not usable when non-root users lack docker group access. Add permanent preflight handling that distinguishes Docker socket permission errors from missing Compose, attempts the standard docker group repair where possible, and prints actionable refresh-shell guidance.
close_reason: Implemented Docker preflight permission handling for non-root users, added actionable docker group repair guidance, and updated CLI tests/installer next steps.

close_reason: Centralized Docker command diagnostics and migrated Docker callers

title: Fix user install layout permission failure
close_reason: Fixed layout log directory creation for non-root installs

title: Implement NocoDB OIDC line uncommenting when authentik selected
description: The step spec says: If authentik is also in foundation_services, uncomment the four NC_OIDC_* lines. But _render_env_example has zero logic for this — lines always stay commented.

title: Add jellyfin as proper optional service
description: Jellyfin exists in registry and templates but is not in the service catalog (03-services.md). Users cannot select it during rakkib init. Its docker-compose also references JELLYFIN_IMAGE which is never set in state.
close_reason: Closed

title: install.sh: auto-install pipx dependencies instead of manual instructions
description: On fresh systems, pipx is not installable via pip because python3-pip/python3-venv are missing. Currently install.sh dies with 'Install pipx manually (https://pypa.github.io/pipx/) and rerun.' We should detect the package manager (apt-get, dnf, brew, etc.), prompt the user, and install the required system packages automatically.
close_reason: Implemented package-manager-aware auto-install for pipx and python3 dependencies in install.sh. Added _detect_package_manager, _install_system_python_deps, and _prompt helpers. Updated E2E tests. All 436 tests pass.

title: Wave 4 — agent escape hatch: agent_handoff.py
description:
Implement agent_handoff.py. On any verify() failure, binary asks "launch agent to diagnose? (Y/n)" and invokes the available agent with: failed step, log tail, relevant state keys, and the specific question files for that step — not AGENT_PROTOCOL.md whole. The agent is reduced to an escape hatch for the parts that genuinely need judgment.

From pyplan.md: Wave 4 — agent escape hatch. Implement agent_handoff.py. On verify() failure, offer to launch agent with narrow prompt.
notes: Implementation complete and tested (255 tests pass). agent_handoff.py fully wired into cli.py. Blocked by Rakkib-8wf (Wave 3 Cloudflare) for end-to-end integration testing.
close_reason: Implementation complete: agent_handoff.py fully wired into cli.py with narrow prompt construction, secret redaction, log tail reading, step-to-file mapping, and agent launch via subprocess. All 255 tests pass. Code committed and pushed to origin/main.

title: Wave 3 — Cloudflare (Step 40): tunnel discovery, DNS, auth handoff
description:
Binary handles tunnel discovery, DNS routing, and the post-login wiring for Step 40 (Cloudflare). For login itself, binary prints the URL and either waits on the local cloudflared callback (browser flow) or hands off to the agent with a narrow prompt for headless / api-token paths. The agent is invoked only for the auth decision, not for the deterministic orchestration.

From pyplan.md: Wave 3 — Cloudflare (Step 40). Binary handles tunnel discovery, DNS routing, and the post-login wiring.
close_reason: Wave 3 complete: cloudflare step wired into CLI (src/rakkib/cli.py), comprehensive tests added (tests/test_steps_cloudflare.py, 20 tests), 275 total tests passing

title: Create slim runtime branch for curl installer
description: The current curl-pipe installer downloads install.sh from main, then install.sh clones the full main branch. That brings dev-only project files onto target servers, including .beads, .claude, .opencode, docs, services planning docs, tests, and web assets. Create a dedicated runtime branch that contains only the files Rakkib needs to run, and update the installer flow so the existing public command still works: curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/FayaaDev/Rakkib/main/install.sh | bash. Proposed approach: create a runtime branch; keep install.sh, pyproject.toml, README.md if useful, .gitignore, and src/rakkib/** including src/rakkib/data/**; remove dev/project files from that branch such as .beads/, .claude/, .opencode/, docs/, services/, tests/, web/, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, WebUI.md, and pyqr.md. Update install.sh on main so BRANCH defaults to runtime instead of main. Update the clone command to use a shallow single-branch clone: git clone --depth 1 --single-branch --branch "$BRANCH" "$REPO_URL" "$INSTALL_DIR". Apply the same install.sh default on the runtime branch so future updates track runtime. Runtime code currently appears package-data driven under src/rakkib/data via pyproject.toml package-data, and rakkib.steps.services resolves templates from Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / data, so src/rakkib/data must stay in the runtime branch. Acceptance criteria: the existing curl command still fetches install.sh from main; default install clones runtime; users can still override with RAKKIB_BRANCH or --branch; git ls-tree -r runtime --name-only shows only runtime-required files; dev-only directories are absent from runtime; bare-metal validation runs bash install.sh in a clean checkout or the curl-pipe command on Ubuntu 24.04 per project rules.
close_reason: Closed

- Create prompt wrapper helpers in interview.py (or tui.py) for text, confirm (2-item select), single_select (select), multi_select (checkbox).

description:
After completing Waves 0-4 of the pyplan.md v2 refactor, update all agent-facing documentation to reflect the new architecture where the Python binary is the primary driver and agents are escape hatches only.

Files to update:
- AGENTS.md: reframe agent as escape hatch, not driver
- CLAUDE.md: same reframe, Claude-specific wording
- AGENT_PROTOCOL.md: demote from runtime prompt to human reference spec, add Agent Escape Hatch Contract section, note binary-owned execution

title: Distribution channel decision: pipx from GitHub release vs PyPI
description:
Decide between pipx install from a tagged GitHub release vs publishing to PyPI. PyPI is cleaner long-term; a GitHub release wheel is fine for v2.0. This is a v2.0 shipping decision that affects install.sh and CI/CD setup.

From pyplan.md: Distribution channel. pipx from a tagged GitHub release vs publishing to PyPI.
close_reason: Decision: v2.0 ships via GitHub release wheel (pipx install from release asset). PyPI deferred to v2.1+. Documented in install.sh header comment and pyplan.md.

title: Verification end-to-end: VM test, token cost, resume, failure, add, idempotent
description:
1. Fresh Ubuntu 24.04 VM: curl … install.sh | bash → installs Python+pipx if missing → rakkib init runs wizard. No LLM during phases 1–6.
2. Token cost: Authentik + Immich install consumes zero LLM tokens for interview and docker compose up.
3. Resume: Kill terminal in Phase 3, relaunch rakkib init → resumes silently. With confirmed: true, prompts "start over? (y/N)" once.
4. Step failure: Force Step 60 failure with bogus image tag → binary offers agent diagnose with narrow context.
5. Post-install add: rakkib add jellyfin → runs only Jellyfin slice.
6. Idempotent re-apply: rakkib init on deployed host is no-op or diff-and-merge.

From pyplan.md: Verification (end-to-end).

Remove bin/rakkib (bash entrypoint), lib/common.sh (logic absorbed into Python modules), scripts/rakkib-doctor (becomes rakkib doctor in the Python CLI). cmd_uninstall logic moves into the Python CLI. AGENT_PROTOCOL.md and questions/*.md stay in repo as human-readable spec but are no longer runtime prompts.

Also covers rakkib doctor --interactive (formerly scripts/rakkib-doctor).

From pyplan.md: Wave 5 — post-install. rakkib add <service> reuses wizard for just that service, runs only Step 60, updates state and README.
